steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Cummings and Knapp contributed to Lullfitz's research. The notes show they confirmed their ancestors carried youlk when traveling — a concrete detail that supported Lullfitz's hypothesis about the plant's distribution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'describes them vaguely': identifies who Cummings and Knapp are without explaining what specific information they provided. ✗
  • Choice B — 'misattributes Lullfitz's findings': credits Cummings and Knapp with conclusions that belong to Lullfitz herself. ✗
  • Choice C — 'focuses on identity, not contribution': tells us about their background rather than what they contributed to the study. ✗
  • Choice D — 'ancestors transported the plant, supporting the hypothesis': states the specific information they gave (ancestors carried youlk) and explains why it mattered to Lullfitz's research. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
